supporting_evidence: Incident breadcrumbs show table-level height animation lifecycle events (`animation.start` -> `animation.batch.*` -> `animation.complete`) while user-visible report says card bottom border snaps on collapse/edit transitions.

Existing repo claim (`estimate-row-detail-frame-height-is-discrete-toggle`) indicates detail reveal has used discrete target-state geometry in at least one render scope, but that does not yet establish whether row/card/detail/child scopes share one driver during the failing transitions.
contradicting_evidence: No incident evidence indicates tentative-vs-committed business state divergence; symptoms are geometric and transition-phase specific.
Subsequent row positions animate, so at least one geometry scope is on an interpolated driver; unknown if card border and detail scopes are phase-aligned with it.
missing_evidence: Per-frame comparative geometry samples for row/container, card border shell, detail/edit region, and children below the expansion boundary during the same transition.
candidates.next_discriminating_check: Instrument one row render path to log synchronized per-frame geometry signals for row/container, card outer+border, detail/edit region, and below-boundary children during expand/collapse/edit transitions; then compare whether those scopes are driven by one interpolated signal or mixed discrete/interpolated drivers.
